Figure 3. Visualization of retrieved prototypes from local and global memory.

Figure 1. Overview of V-HMN. (a) Each HMN block refines features through local and global Hopfield memory retrieval, rather than convolution or self-attention. (b) A deep backbone is constructed by stacking HMN blocks, with attention pooling and a linear head for image classification.
Figure 4. Effect of β initialization on model accuracy with refinement iteration fixed to t = 1.

Table 8. Performance under class-imbalance on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100. All results are top-1 accuracy (%). Baselines include ViT (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021), Swin-ViT (Liu et al., 2021), MLP-Mixer (Tolstikhin et al., 2021), MetaFormer (Yu et al., 2024), Vim (Zhu et al., 2024), and AiT (Sun et al., 2025). Results are reported as mean ± standard deviation over 3 seeds.
Figure 6. Robustness analysis of V-HMN’s associative memory under Gaussian noise and occlusion.
